visible_observations: A mature broad-shouldered goblin-like man stands with pointed ears, swept spiky hair, dark patterned skin, layered black scale-and-strap garments, wrapped boots, and a sly closed-mouth expression.
Both hands operate an oversized wooden stamp above a long printed paper strip, with one hand pressing the handle and the other guiding the document.
His attention and weight lean into the stamping action, making administrative control or judgment more visually central than a conventional weapon.
strengths: The stamp-and-ledger mechanism is a specific dominant prop that separates the figure from ordinary swordsmen and ties face, hands, and posture to one action.
Pointed ears, patterned skin, compact muscular frame, layered shoulder scales, and spiked hair create a mature, physically credible nonhuman individual.
The dark dry materials, worn paper, wood, and metal rings remain restrained rather than glossy or brightly magical.
defects: The active pose reads as clerk, tax collector, merchant, or bureaucrat before it reads as combatant because neither the stamp nor paper visibly harms, restrains, or targets anyone.
Dense repeated black leather scales, crosshatching, straps, and tabs flatten garment hierarchy and merge the torso, skirt, legs, and boots into one dark patterned field.
No visible wound, corruption, compulsion, or bodily consequence distinguishes the prop's power from ordinary institutional authority.
uncertainties: Unaided inspection cannot determine whether the stamp is a weapon, magical contract tool, official seal, or mundane comic prop.
The paper's target, stakes, and effect are not visible within the master.

visible_observations: Three alternatives show the same pointed-eared, spiky-haired, layered-skirt humanoid stamping a paper strip from a left lean, a frontal planted stance, and a wide rightward lunge.
The paper curls differently and the stance changes center of gravity, but body plan, costume mass, prop, hand relationship, and overall role remain constant.
strengths: Candidate B gives the clearest separate read of head, shoulders, hands, stamp, bright paper, planted legs, and ragged lower garment.
Candidate C introduces the strongest lateral action and Candidate A the most covert hunched posture.
defects: The sheet explores three pose variants of one design rather than materially different silhouette concepts; none changes anatomy, prop class, garment family, or dominant visual premise.
The stamp is small in all three outer contours, leaving a familiar spiky goblin or rogue silhouette until the internal paper shape is inspected.
uncertainties: The sheet does not show how the paper or stamp alters the exterior silhouette during an actual attack or ability.

visible_observations: The mature nonhuman face angles downward toward the stamp with one eye narrowed almost shut, the other shaded beneath a heavy brow, cheeks lifted, and the mouth pulled into a restrained asymmetric smile.
Both hands remain visible around the large stamp handle, so eye direction, head tilt, and expression are tied to the act of approving or condemning the paper.
strengths: The half-lidded eye, compressed cheek, crooked smile, and focused hand action communicate private satisfaction and calculating attention rather than a neutral portrait.
Wrinkled skin, broad nose, pointed ears, scars or engraved facial lines, and distinct hair produce a specific adult face.
defects: The smile can read as benign craft pride or comic bureaucratic pleasure as readily as coercive intent because the affected person and consequence are absent.
Eye detail is heavily shadowed and the expression stays close to a familiar sly goblin smirk, limiting a more complex fear or contradiction.
uncertainties: The unaided face does not establish whether he is amused, greedy, vindictive, meticulous, or simply pleased with completed work.

visible_observations: At native size, pointed ears, spiked hair, broad shoulders, bent stamping arms, separated legs, and the bright curling paper remain visible.
The stamp handle is only a small dark knob; facial expression, skin pattern, scales, straps, printed marks, and garment layers collapse into the nearly black body.
strengths: The white paper supplies a strong action cue and gives the otherwise dark figure an identifiable asymmetric extension.
The planted legs and compact upper body preserve a stable humanoid shape.
defects: The figure reads first as a dark goblin holding paper because the stamp and its pressing action are too small to resolve clearly.
Repeated dark materials merge the torso, arms, skirt, and boots, while the face and sly attention vanish.
uncertainties: The native image alone does not establish whether players would identify an ability involving a stamp or distinguish the paper from a scroll, banner, or loose cloth.
